summary: Completed initial testing of Google Drive Slideshow app. Found critical backend API configuration issue preventing public Drive folder access. Frontend UI loads correctly but gets stuck due to backend timeouts.

critical_bugs.issue: API timeouts due to missing Google API key for public Drive access
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks all public folder functionality - core feature unusable
critical_bugs.details: Backend builds Google Drive service with developerKey=None, causing API calls to hang. Need to add GOOGLE_API_KEY to environment variables.

critical_bugs.issue: API timeouts on authenticated folder access

working_endpoints.status: Working correctly - returns 200 with expected message
Working correctly - returns OAuth URL and session ID

ui_bugs.issue: Button gets stuck in 'Loading...' state due to backend API timeout
ui_bugs.details: Button becomes disabled and shows loading state but never recovers due to backend timeout

ui_bugs.issue: Button becomes disabled after Load button is clicked and stays disabled
ui_bugs.details: Button should remain clickable even if other operations are in progress

integration_issues.issue: Frontend makes API call but hangs waiting for backend response

passed_tests: Landing page loads correctly with proper Swiss minimal design
Title and subtitle display correctly using Cabinet Grotesk and IBM Plex Sans fonts
Drive link input field is functional and accepts text input
Load public button is present and clickable
Google auth button is present and visible
Backend root API endpoint (/api/) returns 200 with correct message
Backend OAuth connect endpoint (/api/drive/connect) returns 200 with authorization URL
UI follows design guidelines with correct colors, typography, and layout

success_percentage.frontend: 70% (UI loads correctly but core functionality blocked by backend)

action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Add Google API key to backend environment variables. The backend needs GOOGLE_API_KEY in .env file for public Drive access. Current implementation uses developerKey=None which causes API timeouts. Also need to handle API timeout errors gracefully in frontend to prevent UI from getting stuck in loading state.
